description:
Goal: Add a sender field to chat messages so the dashboard can render Architect and Shepherd messages with distinct bubble colors.

Specs:
- In lib/claude-chat.js pushHistory(): accept optional sender parameter (default "user" for role=user, "assistant" for role=assistant)
- In lib/gemini.js equivalent history functions: same sender parameter
- Persist sender in the history JSON: each message object gets a "sender" field
  Valid values: "user", "assistant", "architect", "shepherd"
- In server.js WebSocket handler: when broadcasting user_message events, include sender field
- In public/gemini.js (frontend): when rendering chat messages, check the sender field
  - "user" → existing user bubble style
  - "assistant" → existing assistant bubble style
  - "architect" → dark green background (#166534), white text
  - "shepherd" → amber/dark orange background (#92400e), white text
- Add CSS classes: .bubble-architect, .bubble-shepherd in the chat CSS
- Backwards compatible: messages without sender field render as before

Verification:
- Server starts without errors: node server.js
- Open dashboard, send a message in a workspace chat — renders as normal user bubble
- Manually POST to /api/chat/:workspace/send with sender:"architect" — message renders with dark green bubble
- Existing chat history (no sender field) still renders correctly

Safety:
- Do NOT break existing chat rendering — sender field is additive
- Do NOT change the WebSocket message protocol shape — only add optional sender field
- Test with an existing workspace that has chat history to verify backwards compat
- Run the server and verify it starts before closing

CHALLENGE:simple
title: Message sender field + colored chat bubbles

description:
Goal: Ensure every worker workspace starts with a clean git worktree, preventing stale beads changes or uncommitted code from previous sessions from confusing the next worker.

Specs:
- In routes/v1.js POST /api/sessions/new: after git.addWorktree(), verify clean state
- In routes/v1.js POST /api/sessions/start (restart existing): add a pre-start cleanup step
  - git reset --hard
  - git clean -fd
  - git fetch origin
  - git checkout -B <branch> origin/main (or origin/<base-branch>)
- Add a helper function cleanWorktree(worktreePath, baseBranch) in lib/git.js
- The helper should be idempotent and safe to call on already-clean worktrees
- Log the cleanup steps for debugging

Verification:
- Create a workspace, make some uncommitted changes in its worktree
- Restart the session via POST /api/sessions/start
- Verify the worktree is clean: git status shows no changes
- New workspaces via POST /api/sessions/new still work as before

Safety:
- Do NOT modify existing worktree creation logic — only add cleanup before session start
- Do NOT delete worktrees — only reset their state
- The cleanup MUST NOT run on the main repo, only on worktree paths
- Add a safety check: refuse to clean if path is the main repo root
- Run the server and verify it starts before closing

CHALLENGE:simple
title: Clean worktree guarantee for worker sessions

close_reason: Done — POST /api/chat/:workspace/send implemented in routes/v1.js with sender field support in pushHistory
description:
Goal: Add a REST endpoint that sends a message to a workspace Claude session, so the Architect and Shepherd can communicate with Workers without using WebSocket.

Specs:
- Add route in routes/v1.js: POST /api/chat/:workspace/send
- Accept JSON body: { message: string, sender?: "user"|"architect"|"shepherd" }
- Default sender to "user" if not provided
- For workspaces with active Claude relay: use claudeChat.appendMessage()
- For workspaces without active relay: use claudeChat.sendMessage() to start new conversation
- Persist the sender field alongside chat history messages
- Return { ok: true, workspace } on success
- Return 404 if workspace/project not found
- Return 400 if message is empty

Verification:
- Server starts without errors: node server.js
- With a running workspace, POST to /api/chat/:workspace/send with {"message":"hello","sender":"architect"} returns 200
- The message appears in the workspace chat history with sender field preserved
- Existing WebSocket chat functionality is unaffected

Safety:
- Do NOT modify the WebSocket handler logic — only add the REST route
- Do NOT change the claudeChat module's public API — only use existing methods
- If claudeChat needs changes for sender tracking, make them additive only
- Run the server and verify it starts before closing

CHALLENGE:simple
title: REST API: POST /api/chat/:workspace/send

close_reason: Done — GET /api/chat/:workspace/status returns relayActive, streaming, chatMode, lastActivity, pendingPermission
description:
Goal: Add a REST endpoint that returns the status of a workspace's chat session, including whether a Claude is active, streaming state, and any pending questions from Bead comments.

Specs:
- Add route in routes/v1.js: GET /api/chat/:workspace/status
- Response JSON:
  {
    workspace: string,
    relayActive: boolean,       // claudeChat.isActive(workspace)
    streaming: boolean,         // workspaceState.isStreaming(workspace)
    chatMode: string,           // workspaceState mode
    lastActivity: number,       // timestamp
    pendingPermission: object|null  // any pending approval request
  }
- Return 404 if workspace/project not found

Verification:
- Server starts without errors: node server.js
- GET /api/chat/some-workspace/status returns 200 with expected shape
- Fields match the actual workspace state

Safety:
- Do NOT modify existing routes — only add new one
- This is a read-only endpoint, no state mutations
- Run the server and verify it starts before closing

CHALLENGE:simple
title: REST API: GET /api/chat/:workspace/status

close_reason: Done — GET /api/beads, GET /api/beads/:id, POST /api/beads, PATCH /api/beads/:id all implemented with bd CLI, input sanitization, and JSON responses
description:
Goal: Add REST endpoints that expose Beads operations so the MCP server (and any HTTP client) can read, create, and update Beads without shelling out to bd.

Specs:
- Add routes in routes/v1.js:
  - GET /api/beads — returns all beads as JSON array (parse .beads/issues.jsonl)
  - GET /api/beads/:id — returns single bead by ID
  - POST /api/beads — create a new bead. Body: { title, description, priority?, type?, deps? }
    Shells out to: bd create "title" --description="desc" -p N -t type --deps ... --json
  - PATCH /api/beads/:id — update bead. Body: { status?, comment?, assignee?, priority? }
    Shells out to: bd update <id> --status X --json (and/or bd comment <id> "text")
- All endpoints return JSON
- Use child_process.execSync for bd commands (synchronous is fine for this)
- Set cwd to the main repo path (config.reposDir + repo name, or a new config field)
- Handle bd command failures gracefully (return 500 with error message)

Verification:
- Server starts without errors: node server.js
- GET /api/beads returns JSON array of existing beads
- POST /api/beads with {title:"test",description:"test bead"} creates a bead and returns it
- PATCH /api/beads/:id with {status:"in_progress"} updates the bead
- GET /api/beads/:id returns the updated bead

Safety:
- Do NOT modify .beads/ files directly — always go through bd CLI
- Do NOT install any new npm packages — use built-in child_process
- Sanitize the bead ID parameter to prevent command injection (alphanumeric + hyphens only)
- Run the server and verify it starts before closing

CHALLENGE:simple
title: REST API: Beads CRUD endpoints

description:
Goal: Establish a trunk-based development workflow where all agents commit directly to main, with fast presubmit tests gating commits and expensive tests running in GitHub Actions CI.

Specs:
Phase A — Presubmit (local, fast):
- Add a pre-push git hook (or pre-commit) that runs fast tests before allowing push to main
- Fast tests = linting, syntax checks, unit tests if any exist
- Must complete in <30 seconds to avoid blocking agents
- Hook should be installed by bd init or a setup script

Phase B — CI (GitHub Actions):
- Add a GitHub Actions workflow on push to main
- Runs the full test suite (integration tests, slower checks)
- Reports pass/fail status
- Keeps a record of which commit broke what

Phase C — Build Sheriff:
- A Claude that runs whenever CI reports a failure on main
- Reads the CI failure log
- Identifies the commit that broke it (git bisect or just the latest push)
- Either: files a fix Bead, or reverts the offending commit
- This is a specialized Shepherd variant — ephemeral, triggered by CI failure

Design notes:
- All workers commit directly to main (trunk-based)
- Worktrees use throwaway branches only to satisfy git worktree constraints, then push to main
- No long-lived feature branches
- Small, verified commits (2-10 min tasks) make reverts cheap
- The build sheriff is the safety net that makes this sustainable

Verification:
- Pre-push hook blocks a push with failing tests
- CI workflow runs on push to main and reports status
- Build sheriff responds to CI failures (can be tested with an intentional break)

Safety:
- Pre-push hook must have a bypass flag for emergencies (--no-verify, documented)
- Build sheriff must NOT force-push or rewrite history — only revert commits or file fix Beads
- CI workflow must not expose secrets in logs

CHALLENGE:complex
title: Trunk-based dev: presubmit tests + CI + build sheriff

description:
Goal: Create a Node.js MCP server that wraps the Klaudii REST API, allowing Claude Code CLI instances (the Architect) to manage workspaces and beads via native tool calls.

Specs:
- Create file: mcp/klaudii-mcp-server.js
- Use @modelcontextprotocol/sdk package (install as dependency)
- Implement these MCP tools:
  1. klaudii_list_workspaces() — GET /api/sessions, return simplified workspace list
  2. klaudii_create_workspace(repo, branch, bead_id?) — POST /api/sessions/new
  3. klaudii_send_message(workspace, message, sender?) — POST /api/chat/:workspace/send
  4. klaudii_get_status(workspace) — GET /api/chat/:workspace/status
  5. klaudii_read_beads(status?, priority?) — GET /api/beads with optional filters
  6. klaudii_create_bead(title, description, priority?, type?, deps?) — POST /api/beads
  7. klaudii_update_bead(id, status?, comment?, assignee?) — PATCH /api/beads/:id
- Server communicates via stdio (standard MCP transport)
- Base URL configurable via KLAUDII_URL env var (default http://localhost:9876)
- Create mcp/package.json with the @modelcontextprotocol/sdk dependency
- Add a README.md in mcp/ with Claude Code MCP config example:
  {
    "mcpServers": {
      "klaudii": {
        "command": "node",
        "args": ["<path>/mcp/klaudii-mcp-server.js"]
      }
    }
  }

Verification:
- cd mcp && npm install succeeds
- node mcp/klaudii-mcp-server.js starts without errors (will wait for stdio input)
- With Klaudii server running, the MCP tools return expected data
- Add the MCP config to a Claude Code instance and verify tools appear

Safety:
- Do NOT modify any existing Klaudii files — this is a new standalone module
- Do NOT bundle or inline the MCP SDK — use npm dependency
- Do NOT hardcode any API keys or secrets
- Keep it simple: plain HTTP fetch to localhost, no auth needed
- Run npm install and verify the server starts before closing

CHALLENGE:simple
title: Klaudii MCP server
